name: Caqueta (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the Amazonian forest and the natural wealth of Caqueta
the rivers that cross the department and the peace of its people
the gold and the wealth of natural resources, shown by a star in the canton

description: Seven equal horizontal stripes alternating green and white (green at top), with a square white canton at the upper hoist bearing a single yellow five-pointed star.
history: Caqueta was created as an Intendancy in 1912 and elevated to a department by the 1981 reform, confirmed in the 1991 Constitution. The flag combines the green of the Amazon and the white of its rivers with a star in the canton evoking the gold and the territory's natural riches.

features.symbolism: alternating green and white stripes representing the rivers and the Amazonian jungle of the department
white canton at the upper hoist bearing a single yellow five-pointed star
the gold and the natural wealth of Caqueta
